Initiation of Visit: This visit was initiated by patient's son.
The chief complaint is needing an in-home healthcare provider. The first contact date with
MEDICAL NECESSITY: This visit with a FTF encounter with the patient in the home is not
Patient is seen in the home because there is a medical necessity for this visit to be
conducted in the patient's environment. The patient suffers from recent with pain
exacerbated by activity which is contraindicated for the patient to be seen in an office
setting today. It is medically necessary and safer for the patient to be seen in the patient's
This is a 93 year old Female seen today for initial visit and establishment of care. PMH
includes HTN, DM type 2, HLD, senile dementia, CKD with recent hyponatremia Patient
is unable to providea complete medical history due to her advance dementia, husband and
son are with patient to provide additonal information. They report patient had been with
home hospice and discharged last week due to improving condition. She had experienced
a recent fall and was found to have significant hyponatremia and corrected with Sodium
tablets 1000mg twice daily. She has an appointment to see her nephrologist at Kidney
Specialists of Southern Nevada this week. Son states her mood has been stable, no
outbursts, no aggression, no signs of depression. She mostly sits in her recliner all day and
watches television. She ambulates with a cane or walker for assistance. Sleep has been
good on Trazodone. Appetite has been fair. No coughing or choking noted when eating.
Their main challenge has been with her personal hygiene, particularly bathing, requiring
family assistance and extensive encouragement to shower approximately every two weeks.
She performs basic washing at sink with assistance. Patient is agreeable to visits from
SH: Lives with husband and son in a 2 storey house, patient stays on the first floor. Non-
LOSARTAN/HCTZ 100-12.5MG TABLET, TAKE 1 TABLET BY MOUTH EVERY
SODIUM CHLORIDE 1000 MG TABLET, TAKE 1 TABLET BY MOUTH IN THE MORNING
AND 1 TABLET BY MOUTH IN THE EVENING.
The patient's speech was normal, sharing conversation with normal laryngeal efforts.
Appropriate mood and affect were seen on exam.
